prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for aniline were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard', density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0 singlet reference geometry from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz. The electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) and nucleophilic index (f⁻) were calculated from Mulliken partial charges of the neutral, singly cationic (+1, doublet), and singly anionic (−1, doublet) states. All three species converged at the DFT level. The most electrophilic site is the ortho carbon (C, atom 3, f⁺ = 2.298); the most nucleophilic is the nitrogen atom (N, atom 1, f⁻ = 0.205). Charge-conservation checks (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000) confirm proper convergence of the ionic states. The dual descriptor reveals ortho/para carbons as net electrophilic and the ipso carbon as net nucleophilic, consistent with aniline's known reactivity pattern.</prose>
